title: odontosoria ferruginea
intro: Odontosoria ferruginea, also known as the rusty forked fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Odontosoria and family Lindsaeaceae. It is primarily found in tropical regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. The medicinal properties of Odontosoria ferruginea are attributed to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, with herbal preparations also including dried and powdered roots for use in poultices and topical applications.
